are in the real estate and mining


business in Spokane. Six children


survive.


Mrs. Frances Nelson, 65, Daven-
port, died Sunday at the Deaconess


hospital, where she had been taken
for medical treatment few hours
before her death.
Leroy, the infant son Of J. w.


Hefling, E2307 Sprague avenue, was
buried this morning at Greenwood
cemetery.


Mrs. N. J. Reynolds, the woman


suffrage orator, will give this week
to work in the Pasco North Yakima
district.


A boarding house at Front and
, Market, being moved off the right


of way of the Milwaukee. stalled on
the tracks Of the Inland and caused
an II hour interruption Of traffic
at that point Passengers We're
transferred around the building.
A suffrage meeting will be held
tonight at the First Universalist
church, Fifth and Howard Albert
H. Brown will be the speaker.
Representatives from the various


women's clubs will meet at the Y.


M. c. A. at 2:30 tomorrow after
noon to make arrangements for


woman's day at the interstate fair.
A business meeting will be held
at the Emmanuel Baptist church
after the services next Sunday
morning, when the question of NAM
ing a pastor will be considered.
A fire which threatened to do
100000 or more damage, was
checked at the 100000 mark early
Sunday morning, when the Musser


lumber mill was destroyed. The


lumber of the Washington Mill Co.


was saved and $4,000 damage was


reported by the Union Iron Works.
James Carroll, II, was taken into


informal custody by the police who


will try and get him a job. He


says he beat his way here from
Madison, Wis.


Robbers held up !. &. Snathem,


E33 Nora avenue, Saturday night
| and took $3.45 in cash and watch.


ellis Sapp, ardent courier Of


Mrs. Weart, nee Jimason, was re-
leased from Jail Saturday night,


after being warned to behave him


self.
